message:
feat(frankensqlite): implement RETURNING * column name resolution

Adds proper column name handling for RETURNING * clauses in INSERT,
UPDATE, and DELETE statements. Previously, RETURNING * would return
rows with positional column names (_c0, _c1, etc.) because frankensqlite
Row objects don't carry column metadata.

Implementation approach:
- Detect RETURNING * in SQL statements
- Extract table name from INSERT INTO, UPDATE, or DELETE FROM
- Query PRAGMA table_info(table_name) to get schema column names
- Use schema-provided names instead of inferring from SQL text

New functions:
- get_returning_star_columns(): Detects RETURNING * and fetches schema
- extract_table_name_for_returning(): Parses table name from DML
- extract_identifier(): Handles quoted and unquoted identifiers
- convert_rows_with_schema(): Uses provided column names when available

Changes to existing flow:
- query_sync() now checks for RETURNING * and fetches schema if needed
- infer_returning_columns() handles the marker format for schema lookup
- Added debug eprintln statements to trace column resolution (useful for
  debugging edge cases in the sqlmodel-macro integration)

This enables proper struct field mapping in sqlmodel-derive macros when
using Model::insert_returning() with RETURNING *.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
